learning_outcomes.description: Identify the physical and geological processes involved in reef development and geomorphology;
Recognize and understand the causes of the topographic, ecological and sedimentary zonation on modern reef tops;
Carry out independent field work on coral reefs and assess sediment production and dispersal processes on modern reefs;
Propose the morphology, internal structure, and geological evolution of modern reefs in relation to their current morphology;
Describe the formation and  evolution of coral cays within the context of global change.
Interpret Earth history and palaeoclimate from fossil reefal limestones by analogy with modern reefs.
Contribute in a professional manner to a team to achieve successful outcomes

assessments.task_description:
The practical workshop assessment tasks are based on the first five in class practicals. Specific details on the requirements for the workshop assessment items will be provided in class and in the practical handouts. Practical workshop reports will be collated and handed in as one assessment item at the due date.

Higher marks will be awarded to students who relate their results and interpretations to concepts and details raised in the lectures and through their broader reading as well as accuracy and specificity of the descriptions and answers. Each practical will have equal weighting to the total marks for this assessment item.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Online submission by Turnitin only by the due date and time. Refer to Blackboard for the submission link. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets are required. Submission via email is not accepted.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
You will be provided with a project scope in Week 3 to be conducted during the Heron Island field trip. During the workshops you can take notes on the methods that can be used in the field and discuss with your teachers what is logistically possible when working on a coral reef. The project proposal is a detailed outline on the planned activities you will conduct with your group in the field. It requires background research, clear methods description, logistical planning, and mapping of the locations of the proposed work. Further details on the structure and requirements for the Project Proposal will be provided in class.

While you can discuss and plan with your group this assessment item is submitted individually.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Online submission by Turnitin only by the due date and time. Refer to Blackboard for the submission link. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets are required. Submission via email is not accepted.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.due_date:
1) Quiz 1: 8/09/2026 12:00 pm

2) Quiz 2: 20/10/2026 12:00 pm

Held in practical session.
assessments.task_description:
To assess your learning across the course there will be two quizzes worth 10% each. Each quiz will consist of multiple choice and short answer questions and will be completed under exam conditions. Each quiz will last for one (1) hour. The quizzes will be conducted in class during the first hour of the schedule practical session. Each quiz will test your knowledge of the content delivered in lectures.

Quiz 1 will test content delivered in weeks 1 - 6.

Quiz 2 will test content delivered in weeks 7 - 12.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person on paper. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: To be handed in at the end of the Quiz time period (1 hour).
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
This assessment task is the report from the work you conducted on Heron Island during your project. Your report will follow the structure of scientific journal articles.

Writing conventions and further instructions will be provided on Learn.UQ and in class.
